description_long: This object is a large, mounted anti-materiel or anti-aircraft rifle system, featuring a long, heavy barrel and a robust, angular shield for operator protection. The rifle is mounted on a tripod base with four stabilizing legs, each ending in a circular footpad for stability on various surfaces. The central body of the weapon is reinforced and includes a prominent breech and loading mechanism, with visible mechanical linkages and adjustment levers. The barrel is extended and thick, suggesting it is designed for high-caliber ammunition and sustained fire. The overall construction is predominantly metallic, with a matte dark finish, and the design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability for urban or military deployment.
description: A large, mounted anti-materiel rifle with a long barrel, angular shield, and four-legged tripod base, finished in matte dark metal.
description_view_0: Front view shows the angular shield, central mounting, and symmetrical tripod legs extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the long barrel, breech mechanism, and the full length of the stabilizing legs.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the shield, mounting structure, and the central support column.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the barrel, loading mechanism, and the extended tripod leg structure.
